title: AI is starting to look more like junior analysts than mere chatbots
author: Infinite-Course8737
selftext:
I tried using AI to do tomorrow’s forex macro research.

It pulled together macro headlines, central bank expectations, relative currency strength, and a few potential volatility drivers for the next session, then organised it into a structured report.

Nothing in there is new if you already follow forex. It’s all standard pre-market work: checking rate expectations, reading news flow, and lining up what could move USD, GBP, etc. You could piece the same thing together manually pretty quickly.

What’s more interesting is less the AI itself and more what it’s compressing. A lot of the routine prep work that usually sits between news, charts, and bias-building is basically just being automated into a single pass.

That doesn’t mean it’s giving you a tradeable edge on its own. It’s still just aggregation and patterning of existing information. But for anyone doing intraday or swing trading off macro context, it does change how quickly you can get from “what happened” to “what matters today.”
